title: FEMA Delays Under Kristi Noem Angered Republicans
summary: Long delays in FEMA assistance brought bipartisan complaints, especially in North Carolina after Hurricane Helene in 2024.

title: As Noem Speaks in Nashville, Senate Swarms Mullin
summary: The remarkable split-screen underscored how abruptly Kristi Noem’s ouster as homeland security secretary had unfolded, even by the standards of President Trump’s fast-paced Washington.

title: Senator Blumenthal Pushes to Investigate Kristi Noem for Perjury
summary: Senator Richard Blumenthal of Connecticut said Democrats had evidence to suggest Kristi Noem lied under oath during a Senate hearing on Tuesday.

title: Analysis Suggests School Was Hit Amid U.S. Strikes on Iranian Naval Base
summary: The Feb. 28 school strike in Minab, which killed dozens, including children, appears to have been part of an attack on an adjacent naval base in southern Iran, where officials said U.S. forces were operating.

title: House Turns Back Bid to Restrict Trump’s War Powers in Iran
summary: A handful of Democrats joined Republicans to defeat an effort to force President Trump to go to Congress for approval to continue using force against Iran, while two G.O.P. lawmakers backed it.

title: Iran delays naming a new leader out of security concerns, officials say.
How the Decision to Start a War Became the President’s
summary: Presidents have sidestepped Congress to launch limited military strikes for decades. Trump’s decision to attack Iran is an aggressive escalation.

title: House Republicans Tell Gonzales to Quit Re-Election Campaign
summary: The call came a day after the Texas congressman admitted to an extramarital affair with an aide, and as he faced a runoff to keep his seat.

title: Lev Parnas Running for Congress as a Democrat in Florida
summary: Mr. Parnas, who once tried to help find damaging information on Hunter Biden, said his background had turned him into “an advocate for speaking truth to power.”

title: An Assertive Supreme Court Turns to Curbing State Courts
summary: Liberal justices accused their colleagues of expanding use of the emergency docket again in two orders issued this week.

title: A Judge’s ‘Battle Royale’ With Trump and the Supreme Court
summary: After repeated but cryptic rebukes from the justices, Judge Brian Murphy last week again ruled against one of the administration’s signature immigration programs.

title: How Jonathan Groff of ‘Just in Time’ Became Broadway’s Leading Man
summary: By transmitting his love of live performance, the “Just in Time” actor has completed his ascendance to full musical stardom.

title: Lindsey Halligan Is Under Investigation by the Florida Bar
summary: The actions of Ms. Halligan, who as a U.S. attorney brought criminal cases against President Trump’s enemies, are under review by the organization that licensed her to practice law.

title: Not Just Oil: In Venezuela, U.S. Interior Secretary Pushes for Mining Access
summary: Venezuela is home to large reserves of rare earths, gold and other valuable minerals that the Trump administration wants to exert more control over.

title: Pentagon Officially Notifies Anthropic It Is a ‘Supply Chain Risk’
summary: Anthropic has said it will sue the Defense Department over the designation, which could prevent the start-up from doing business with the U.S. government.

title: Pokémon Company Objects to White House’s Political Memes
summary: “No permission was granted for the use of our intellectual property,” the company said. The Trump administration frequently promotes policies with content from video games.

title: After Apalachee High School Shooting, a Hunger for Accountability
summary: The country is casting an ever wider net in its search for accountability after mass shootings. Charging parents is an emerging tactic.

title: Senator Tim Sheehy Helps Officers Forcibly Remove Protesting Veteran From Hearing
summary: The physical struggle, involving Senator Tim Sheehy of Montana, was captured on video. The protester, Brian McGinnis, said on social media that his arm was broken.

title: A Killing Spree in Utah Rattles a Region and Puzzles Officials
summary: Three women were slain in two locations, officials said on Thursday. A frantic manhunt involving four states led to an arrest, but a motive was still unclear.

title: How $800 Monthly Car Payments Are Hurting Car Sales
summary: Cars have become so expensive that many Americans are putting off or not buying new cars, hurting the auto industry.

title: Nature Report, Killed by Trump, Is Released Independently
summary: A draft assessment of the health of nature in the United States is grim but shot through with bright spots and possibility.

title: Should New York City Burn Its Parks? This Scientist Thinks So.
summary: An unusual outbreak of wildfires in city parks gave scientists a chance to study these rare events. Now they’re coming to different conclusions.

title: Feeling the Effects of 260,000 Federal Jobs Lost
summary: One year in, assessing budget cuts to federal climate and science jobs.

title: Fewer Pregnant Women Received Acetaminophen in E.R.s After White House Warnings
summary: A new analysis of prescription data found that emergency room orders for acetaminophen for pregnant women fell for weeks after the federal warning.

title: The Boys in Trueblood Came to Rock
summary: The four teenage brothers mostly play the oldies. (To them, the oldies are Coldplay and Maroon 5.)

title: Politicians Are Trying to Control the News
summary: The rising global attacks on the free press reveal increasingly desperate regimes attempting to snuff out attempts to find the truth.

title: Mass Hysteria. Thousands of Jobs Lost. Just How Bad Is It Going to Get?
summary: Michael Steinberger on how A.I.’s impact on white-collar jobs may transform politics and society.

title: Trump Fires His Homeland Security Secretary
summary: Also, child care costs are rising fast. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

title: Superfood Fuels Mating Frenzy for Critically Endangered Kakapo
summary: Kakapos, which are reclusive and flightless and can live as long as humans, are found only in New Zealand. They feed on the fruit of the rimu tree.

title: Racist, Antisemitic Slurs Surface in Florida GOP Group Chat
summary: After the secretary of the Miami-Dade County Republican Party created the chat for college students, it devolved into slurs against Black and Jewish people.

title: António Lobo Antunes, One of Europe’s Most Revered Writers, Dies at 83
summary: In a career studded with literary awards, he was the author of dozens of books that grappled with his nation’s legacy of dictatorship and colonialism.

title: Britney Spears Is Arrested on Suspicion of D.U.I. in California
summary: “Hopefully this can be the first step in long overdue change that needs to occur in Britney’s life,” a representative for the pop star said after she was released from jail.
